title: Migrate durable task and resource stores across deployed schemas
goal: Restore durable task and resource operations after a runtime or code upgrade by detecting deployed SQLite schema revisions, applying explicit crash-safe forward migrations and preserving tasks, attempts, leases and audit identity without silently accepting unknown or corrupt stores.

approval.note: Implement schema detection and migration only. Never discard, recreate or downgrade a live store without a verified backup and explicit recovery evidence.

acceptance.assertion: Task and resource stores expose their observed schema version, code-supported versions and required migration path before any mutation; unknown, future or corrupt schemas fail closed with a bounded recovery instruction.
Supported old schemas migrate under an exclusive store lock in explicit transactions with a verified pre-migration backup, idempotent retry and post-migration integrity checks.

Migration preserves task IDs, attempts, terminal outcomes, outcome_unknown state, resource leases, expiry data, owner identity, argv digests and audit references exactly unless a documented schema transformation requires an equivalence proof.
A stale runtime cannot write a newer store. Rolling deployment behavior is specified and tested so incompatible readers fail before changing records.
Fixtures cover every supported historical schema, already-current stores, interrupted migration, concurrent openers, future versions, malformed metadata, backup failure and byte-stable no-op reruns.

After merge and deployment, copies of the production task and resource stores migrate successfully, then live task list/start/status and resource lease operations succeed without record loss; exact runtime and receipt hashes are retained.

metadata.source: Weltgewebe OS T003 Bureau closeout on heim-pc

live_reproduction.task_error: Unsupported task database schema; upgrade Grabowski before listing tasks.
live_reproduction.resource_error: Unsupported resource database schema; upgrade Grabowski before using resource leases.
live_reproduction.observed_consequence: General durable dispatch and SSH-host artifact reconstruction became unavailable during the closeout while narrower Git and GitHub adapters still worked.

metadata.relationship_to_existing: T061 measures compatibility as a deferred hardening topic. T062 records the now-reproduced production incompatibility and requires an explicit migration path.
does_not_establish: that either live database is corrupt
permission to delete or recreate a store
permission to weaken fail-closed schema checks
that all future schemas are automatically compatible

additional_live_reproduction.source: Schauwerk PR #100 merge and Miro closeout
additional_live_reproduction.task_error: Unsupported task database schema; upgrade Grabowski before listing tasks.
additional_live_reproduction.resource_error: Unsupported resource database schema; upgrade Grabowski before using resource leases.
additional_live_reproduction.handling: No database migration, recreation or schema repair was attempted; narrower live adapters were used with independent readback.

current_live_status.task_store: Task list/start/status surfaces operate successfully with the current schema-v2 store.
current_live_status.resource_store: Resource list/acquire/inspect surfaces operate successfully with the current schema-v2 store.
current_live_status.previous_error: The previously recorded unsupported-schema errors are no longer reproducible in the deployed runtime.
current_live_status.mutation_boundary: No database migration, recreation, downgrade or record rewrite was performed during this verification.
current_live_status.remaining_acceptance: Explicit historical-schema migrations, verified backups, interrupted/concurrent migration fixtures, mixed-version write prevention and production-copy preservation proof remain required before verification.

metadata.verification_note: PR #298 ported the crash-safe task/resource store migrations onto current Grabowski main, passed the complete validation suite, merged through an exact head-bound squash, deployed from a clean detached source and was verified live with current, write-compatible task schema 5 and resource schema 2 stores.

validated_acceptance.version-inventory: PASS: Installed runtime exposes bounded read-only schema inventories. Tasks report current schema 5 with supported versions 1-5; resources report current schema 2 with supported versions 1-2. Both report no migration required, no mutation performed and write compatibility.
validated_acceptance.crash-safe-migration: PASS: Exclusive directory locks, BEGIN IMMEDIATE transactions, verified create-only SQLite backups, directory fsync, idempotent backup reuse and rollback are implemented and covered by migration, backup-failure, interrupted-open and concurrent-opener tests.
validated_acceptance.semantic-preservation: PASS: Historical schema fixtures verify preservation of task IDs, attempts, terminalization and outcome state plus resource owner, lease, expiry and metadata identity. The live stores remain operational and current after deployment.
validated_acceptance.mixed-version-boundary: PASS: Future, corrupt, incomplete and ambiguous schemas fail before mutation; rolling-upgrade inventory explicitly marks pre-T062 overlap with future schemas unsupported and requires a full runtime drain.
validated_acceptance.tests: PASS: 2,351 full tests passed with two expected skips; focused task/resource tests and policy, generated-contract, runtime-lock, deploy-tooling and secret gates all passed.
validated_acceptance.live-proof: PASS: Exact merge commit 49de1b26 was deployed from a clean detached, leased source. Runtime health is green and live task/resource inventories report current write-compatible schemas without inventory mutation; subsequent task and resource operations, including the isolated writer-handoff proof, completed against the deployed stores. No claim is made that the already-current production stores required a new backup during this deployment.
